name: Sikkim
landmark: Kanchenjunga views and Khangchendzonga National Park
stopoverNote: Use Bagdogra (IXB) or New Jalpaiguri (NJP) as the reliable gateway; Pakyong is closer to Gangtok but mountain-weather cancellations are common. All travel inside Sikkim is by narrow mountain roads, and North/East Sikkim permit zones must be handled by registered operators.

diet.vegetarian: Vegetarian food is widely available across Sikkim: veg momos, thukpa, dal bhat, aloo dum, sel roti and gundruk are common in Gangtok, Pelling and most homestays. Butter tea and paneer dishes are vegetarian but dairy-heavy, so clarify if you avoid milk products.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is manageable in Gangtok and Pelling but harder in remote North Sikkim, where package homestays serve fixed meals and often use ghee, butter tea or paneer. Pre-request oil-only cooking and simple rice, gundruk jhol, aloo dum and steamed veg momos when booking Lachung/North Sikkim stays.
phrases: Maas bina, shakahari khana dinuhos = please give vegetarian food without meat
Ghee, dudh, paneer narakhnu hola = please do not add ghee, milk or paneer
Tel ma matra pakaunu hola = please cook only in oil

cities.name: Gangtok
cities.notes: Sikkim's energetic capital — MG Marg, monasteries, ropeway views and the logistics base for Tsomgo Lake, Baba Mandir and Nathula Pass permits.

cities.name: Lachung
cities.notes: Remote North Sikkim village used as the permitted base for Yumthang Valley, hot springs, Zero Point and optional Gurudongmar/Lachen extensions.

cities.name: Ravangla
cities.notes: Quiet South Sikkim ridge town with the giant Tathagata Buddha, monasteries and broad Kanchenjunga views; works well as a one-night scenic bridge toward Pelling.

cities.name: Pelling
cities.notes: West Sikkim's classic Kanchenjunga-view base — Skywalk, Chenrezig statue, Pemayangtse Monastery, Rabdentse ruins and sacred Khecheopalri Lake.

itinerary.sim: Indian prepaid SIMs from Jio or Airtel work well in Gangtok and Pelling; coverage becomes patchy in North Sikkim valleys, so download maps and keep operator/driver numbers offline.

Gangtok.name: Gangtok
Gangtok.note: Three days gives one easy arrival/acclimatization day, one monastery-and-city day, and one high-altitude Tsomgo/Nathula permit excursion.

days.note: Bagdogra/NJP to Gangtok is a 4–5 hour mountain transfer, so keep the first day light. Confirm East/North Sikkim permit paperwork with a registered operator before attempting Tsomgo, Nathula or North Sikkim.

activities.name: MG Marg pedestrian promenade
activities.tip: Use the car-free promenade for food, ATMs and cash before remote North Sikkim.

activities.name: Hotel check-in and permit document check
activities.tip: Carry Voter ID or Passport and passport photos; Aadhaar is rejected at army checkposts.

days.note: Rumtek is about 24 km from central Gangtok, so start early and use a local taxi for the monastery circuit. This is the easiest culture day before high-altitude travel.

activities.name: Rumtek Monastery
activities.tip: Photography may be restricted inside the prayer hall.

activities.name: Gangtok Ropeway
activities.tip: Runs roughly 9am–6pm from Deorali station; views are weather-dependent.

days.theme: Tsomgo Lake, Baba Mandir & Nathula Pass
days.note: Inner Line/Protected Area Permit is mandatory and must be arranged through a registered Sikkim operator; no same-day walk-in permits. Nathula is closed Mon/Tue, foreigners cannot visit Nathula/Baba Mandir, and the 14,140 ft altitude can cause breathlessness — walk slowly and descend if unwell.

activities.name: Tsomgo (Changu) Lake
activities.tip: Carry warm layers even in May; yak rides are optional and extra.

activities.name: Baba Harbhajan Mandir
activities.tip: Indian nationals only; follow army instructions.

activities.name: Nathula Pass
activities.cost: Included in permit+cab; additional permit fee ~₹200/person
activities.tip: Closed Mon/Tue and subject to military/weather closures.

meals: Simple MG Marg dinner after return

days.theme: Buffer day for weather or permit slippage
days.note: Keep this day only if you are travelling in shoulder season or want a safety buffer; high passes and NH-10 can close after snow, rain, landslides or military movement.

activities.name: Reserve day for delayed Tsomgo/Nathula permit trip

Lachung.name: Lachung
Lachung.note: Two days covers the classic Gangtok–Lachung–Yumthang loop; a third day is only for fit travellers adding Lachen/Gurudongmar with a proper PAP extension.

days.note: Protected Area Permit is mandatory for North Sikkim and must be QR-issued through a registered operator. Expect 5–7 hours of winding mountain roads, permit checkposts, landslide delays and basic but warm homestay accommodation.

activities.name: Teesta Valley and Singhik viewpoint drive
activities.tip: Start by 7am; carry cash because Lachung has no reliable ATM.

activities.name: Meal-inclusive Lachung homestay night
activities.tip: Confirm vegetarian/vegan needs when booking the package.

hotels.name: Lachung homestay/package hotel
hotels.budget: usually included in North Sikkim package

meals: Butter tea if you take dairy

days.note: Yumthang sits around 11,693 ft; Zero Point is about 15,300 ft and should be attempted only in clear weather by acclimatized travellers. Return to Gangtok/Ravangla routing makes this a very long day if you do not spend another night.

activities.name: Yumthang Valley
activities.tip: April–May is the rhododendron bloom; walk slowly at altitude.

activities.name: Zero Point (Yumesamdong)
activities.tip: Skip if roads are snowy, if you feel unwell, or if travelling with young children/elderly visitors.

activities.name: Return drive toward Gangtok
activities.tip: Build buffer time for checkposts and landslides.

hotels.name: Lachung homestay/package hotel
hotels.budget: usually included in North Sikkim package

hotels.name: Gangtok transit hotel if breaking the return

days.theme: Optional Lachen and Gurudongmar Lake extension
days.note: Gurudongmar Lake at about 17,800 ft is a serious AMS risk and requires a separate Lachen overnight plus PAP extension. Spend only 20–30 minutes at the lake, avoid exertion, and descend immediately with headache, vomiting, confusion or breathlessness; foreigners are not allowed.

activities.name: Gurudongmar Lake
activities.tip: Carry oxygen if high-risk and consult a doctor for children, elderly, pregnant travellers or cardiac/respiratory conditions.

Ravangla.name: Ravangla
Ravangla.note: One night is enough for Buddha Park, Ralang Monastery and a Namchi stop en route; add a second only for a slower ridge-town break.

days.theme: Buddha Park, Namchi & South Sikkim views
days.note: No permit is required for Indian nationals on the South Sikkim/Ravangla route. If coming from Lachung the same day, this leg is tiring because you normally return via Gangtok first; start early or break the journey in Gangtok if roads are delayed.

activities.name: Tathagata Tsal (Buddha Park)
activities.tip: Open roughly 9am–5pm; morning light is best for mountain views.

activities.name: Namchi Siddheshwar Dham (Char Dham) en route
activities.tip: Better as a stop on the Gangtok/Ravangla approach than as a separate day.

days.note: Use this optional day only if you want a quiet mountain pause or if roads/weather pushed your previous transfer late.

activities.name: Repeat Buddha Park for clear Kanchenjunga views
Café and rest before Pelling transfer

Pelling.name: Pelling
Pelling.note: Two days covers sunrise views, the Skywalk/monastery/ruins cluster and Khecheopalri Lake; a third is for Yuksom or a slower departure.

days.note: All major sights are within about 5 km, but Rabdentse requires a short uphill forest walk. Clear sunrise views are never guaranteed; Oct–Nov and Mar–May mornings are the best odds.

activities.name: Kanchenjunga sunrise from hotel terrace/viewpoint
activities.tip: Book a west/northwest-facing room if views matter.

activities.name: Pelling Skywalk and Chenrezig Statue
activities.tip: Open roughly 8am–5pm; wear non-slip shoes.

activities.name: Pemayangtse Monastery
activities.tip: Do not miss the Zangdopelri model on the top floor.

days.note: Khecheopalri is about 45 minutes from Pelling and Singshore Bridge is another mountain-road excursion; combine them if starting by 8am. For same-day NJP departure, do Khecheopalri only and leave early.

activities.name: Khecheopalri Lake
activities.tip: No motorboats; keep the sacred lake quiet and clean.

activities.name: Singshore Bridge
activities.tip: The suspension bridge can feel wobbly and crowded on weekends.

days.note: Use this optional day for a deeper West Sikkim history loop, or keep it as a buffer before the 5–6 hour road transfer back to NJP/Bagdogra.

connections.method: Private taxi/Sikkim-registered package SUV via Mangan and Chungthang
Return package vehicle to Gangtok, then private taxi toward Ravangla via Namchi
connections.cost: Lachung return included in package; Gangtok→Ravangla ₹2,500–₹4,500

connections.method: Private taxi on South/West Sikkim mountain roads

extras: All East/North Sikkim protected areas — Tsomgo, Nathula, Baba Mandir, Lachung, Yumthang, Zero Point and Gurudongmar — require permits arranged by registered operators; no walk-in same-day permit should be assumed.
Carry Voter ID or Passport plus passport photos for permits; Aadhaar is rejected at army checkposts according to the 2026 permit guidance in the research.

North Sikkim, Tsomgo and Nathula are weather- and army-control dependent; landslides, snow or military movement can close routes without warning, so keep a buffer day if these are must-do experiences.
Gurudongmar is an optional extension, not part of the core route: it needs a Lachen overnight, PAP extension and careful AMS judgement at 17,800 ft.
For departure, Pelling to NJP/Bagdogra is a 5–6 hour mountain-road transfer; leave by 6–7am for afternoon trains or flights.
